title: Ensure Cloud Object Storage Encryption is set to On with  BYOK

input_row.title: Ensure Cloud Object Storage Encryption is set to On with  BYOK
input_row.description: You can use IBM Cloud encryption key management service, for example Key Protect, to bring your own root key (BYOK) to IBM Cloud and use it to add envelope encryption for data that is stored in IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets.
input_row.rationale: When it comes to encryption of data at rest in IBM Cloud, the best practice is to allow customers to manage customer root keys (CRK) that are used to protect customer data stored in data and storage services in IBM Cloud. Bring your Own Key (BYOK) allows customers to ensure no one outside of their organization has access to the root key and and with the support of BYOK, customers can manage the lifecycle of their customer root keys where they can create, rotate, delete those keys. This provides a significant level of control where those CRKs are managed by the customer, which in turn increases both security control as well as meet relevant compliance requirements. These CRKs can be used in turn to protect the data encryption keys used to encrypt the data.
input_row.audit: You can use the IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket configuration properties to verify whether a Cloud Object Storage bucket is enabled to use Key Protect. From Console: Navigate to your Cloud Object Storage instance: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com 2. Click the Menu icon and select Resource List 3. On the Resource List page under Storage, select the Cloud Object Storage instance that you have provisioned. 4. Click on the appropriate bucket that you would like to check 5. Click on Configuration and scroll down to Associated key management service to check/confirm if a Key Protect key management service is associated
input_row.remediation: You will not be able to add Key Protect as the key management service once data is already written to a Cloud Object Storage bucket. In order to ensure that objects are encrypted using Key Protect root keys you will need to create a new Cloud Object Storage bucket, set it to use Key Protect key management service and then upload/copy the existing objects to this new bucket.
input_row.impact: For Bring your Own Key (BYOK) encryption with Cloud Object Storage integration and Key Protect key management service you will need to review and understand how you can leverage the integration to handle key lifecycle events (for example key rotation, deletion, restore). Refer to the Cloud Object Storage integration with Key Protect section to learn about key lifecycle management and Key Protect product page to find out more about the key lifecycle events.
input_row.references: 1. • Data encryption with IBM Cloud Object Storage: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage- encryption 2. • Integrated service availability page: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/services/cloud- object-storage/basics?topic=cloud-object-storage-service-availability 3. • https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/services/cloud-object-storage/basics?topic=cloud- object-storage-compatibility-api-bucket-operations#compatibility-api-key-protect
input_row.default_value: By default, use of Key Protect encryption key management service with IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets is not enabled.
input_row.additional_information: Creating a Cloud Object Storage bucket with Key Protect (Bring Your Own Key): 1. From your Cloud Object Storage instance page, create a new Cloud Object Storage bucket by clicking Create bucket. 2. To create the bucket, in the Create bucket section, select Custom bucket. 3. In the bucket configuration section, you can select the option to use Key Protect under Key management services Note: In order to configure a Cloud Object Storage bucket to use Key Protect key management service you will need an instance of Key Protect along with appropriate access authorization to your Cloud Object Storage instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Log in to IBM Cloud**:
   - Navigate to https://cloud.ibm.com and log in to your account.

2. **Access Cloud Object Storage**:
   - Click the Menu icon, and select **Resource List**.
   - Under the **Storage** section, select your **Cloud Object Storage** instance.

3. **Select the Bucket**:
   - On your Cloud Object Storage instance page, locate and click on the appropriate bucket that you want to evaluate.

4. **Check Bucket Configuration**:
   - Click on **Configuration**.
   - Scroll down to find **Associated key management service**.

5. **Verify Key Protect Integration**:
   - Confirm whether a **Key Protect** key management service is associated with the bucket. If a service is listed, this indicates that the bucket is set to use Key Protect for encryption.

6. **Expected Secure Configuration**:
   - The bucket should have **Key Protect** listed as the key management service. If it does not, this indicates that BYOK encryption is not set to On.

7. **Confirm Compliance**:
   - If the bucket is not using Key Protect, note this down for remediation. If it is, the compliance for this control can be considered satisfied.
